sources: Sanitized benchmark summaries curated from internal validation runs
Benchmark methodology and scenario notes maintained in this repository

astra_vs_etcd.astra_summary: 7267.42 RPS, 47.52 ms p99, 50k/50k writes, no OOM
astra_vs_etcd.etcd_summary: 980.17 RPS on the same run shape
astra_vs_etcd.notes: This was the first public-quality proof that Astra could beat etcd decisively under the constrained-disk scenario that originally blocked the project.

astra_vs_etcd.workload: kwok-driven K3s churn at ~17k pod realization
astra_vs_etcd.astra_summary: PUT 335.25 ms p99, GET 312.17 ms p99, LIST 2952.64 ms p99, 1001 nodes / 17506 pods / 10001 configmaps
astra_vs_etcd.etcd_summary: PUT 158.11 ms p99, GET 143.06 ms p99, LIST 2728.32 ms p99, 1001 nodes / 16113 pods / 10001 configmaps
astra_vs_etcd.notes: Astra was stable but materially slower than etcd for this early control-plane workload shape.

astra_vs_etcd.astra_summary: PUT 151.13 ms p99, GET 117.06 ms p99, LIST 268.02 ms p99, peak disk IOPS 30.0, iowait p99 5.0%
astra_vs_etcd.etcd_summary: PUT 106.01 ms p99, GET 108.01 ms p99, LIST 259.00 ms p99, peak disk IOPS 22.0, iowait p99 16.5%
astra_vs_etcd.notes: This run closed the biggest K3s acceptance gaps: GET decoupling, LIST parity, iowait harmony, and 30k pod realization.

feature_validations.scenario: QoS, governor, ecosystem, and gray-failure suite
feature_validations.summary: Gateway storm reached 13447.49 RPS with 0.0253% error rate; Patroni lease updates stayed at 33.02 ms p99; gray-failure delta gate passed with stable leader behavior.

feature_validations.scenario: Migration DX and Omni fleet cutover
feature_validations.summary: 5 sources converged with 5/5 tenant parity matches; 5/5 real Omni external-etcd boots passed with no key loss.
50k watch fanout passed at 4995.20 write RPS and 150 ms lag p99; semantic cache showed 1.299x RPS gain and 1.292x p99 improvement; multi-raft prefix sharding reached 2.009x scale gain.
